NKJV.1: But as for you, speak the things which are proper for sound doctrine:
NKJV.2: that the older men be sober, reverent, temperate, sound in faith, in love, in patience;
NKJV.3: the older women likewise, that they be reverent in behavior, not slanderers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things—
NKJV.4: that they admonish the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,
NKJV.5: to be discreet, chaste, homemakers, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God may not be blasphemed.
NKJV.6: Likewise, exhort the young men to be sober-minded,
NKJV.7: in all things showing yourself to be a pattern of good works; in doctrine showing integrity, reverence, incorruptibility,
NKJV.8: sound speech that cannot be condemned, that one who is an opponent may be ashamed, having nothing evil to say of you.
NKJV.9: Exhort bondservants to be obedient to their own masters, to be well pleasing in all things, not answering back,
NKJV.10: not pilfering, but showing all good fidelity, that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Savior in all things.
NKJV.11: For the grace of God that brings salvation has appeared to all men,
NKJV.12: teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly in the present age,
NKJV.13: looking for the blessed hope and glorious appearing of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ,
NKJV.14: who gave Himself for us, that He might redeem us from every lawless deed and purify for Himself His own special people, zealous for good works.
NKJV.15: Speak these things, exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no one despise you.

ESV.1: But as for you, teach what accords with sound  doctrine.
ESV.2: Older men are to be sober-minded, dignified, self-controlled, sound in faith, in love, and in steadfastness.
ESV.3: Older women likewise are to be reverent in behavior, not slanderers or slaves to much wine. They are to teach what is good,
ESV.4: and so train the young women to love their husbands and children,
ESV.5: to be self-controlled, pure, working at home, kind, and submissive to their own husbands, that the word of God may not be reviled.
ESV.6: Likewise, urge the younger men to be self-controlled.
ESV.7: Show yourself in all respects to be a model of good works, and in your teaching show integrity, dignity,
ESV.8: and sound speech that cannot be condemned, so that an opponent may be put to shame, having nothing evil to say about us.
ESV.9: Bondservants  are to be submissive to their own masters in everything; they are to be well-pleasing, not argumentative,
ESV.10: not pilfering, but showing all good faith, so that in everything they may adorn the doctrine of God our Savior.
ESV.11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation for all people,
ESV.12: training us to renounce ungodliness and worldly passions, and to live self-controlled, upright, and godly lives in the present age,
ESV.13: waiting for our blessed hope, the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ,
ESV.14: who gave himself for us to redeem us from all lawlessness and to purify for himself a people for his own possession who are zealous for good works.
ESV.15: Declare these things; exhort and rebuke with all authority. Let no one disregard you.

NASB.1: But as for you, speak the things which are fitting for sound doctrine.
NASB.2: Older men are to be temperate, dignified, sensible, sound in faith, in love, in perseverance.
NASB.3: Older women likewise are to be reverent in their behavior, not malicious gossips nor enslaved to much wine, teaching what is good,
NASB.4: so that they may encourage the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,
NASB.5: [to be] sensible, pure, workers at home, kind, being subject to their own husbands, so that the word of God will not be dishonored.
NASB.6: Likewise urge the young men to be sensible;
NASB.7: in all things show yourself to be an example of good deeds, [with] purity in doctrine, dignified,
NASB.8: sound [in] speech which is beyond reproach, so that the opponent will be put to shame, having nothing bad to say about us.
NASB.9: [Urge] bondslaves to be subject to their own masters in everything, to be well-pleasing, not argumentative,
NASB.10: not pilfering, but showing all good faith so that they will adorn the doctrine of God our Savior in every respect.
NASB.11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,
NASB.12: instructing us to deny ungodliness and worldly desires and to live sensibly, righteously and godly in the present age,
NASB.13: looking for the blessed hope and the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Christ Jesus,
NASB.14: who gave Himself for us to redeem us from every lawless deed, and to purify for Himself a people for His own possession, zealous for good deeds.
NASB.15: These things speak and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one disregard you.

NLT.1: As for you, Titus, promote the kind of living that reflects wholesome teaching.
NLT.2: Teach the older men to exercise self-control, to be worthy of respect, and to live wisely. They must have sound faith and be filled with love and patience.
NLT.3: Similarly, teach the older women to live in a way that honors God. They must not slander others or be heavy drinkers. Instead, they should teach others what is good.
NLT.4: These older women must train the younger women to love their husbands and their children,
NLT.5: to live wisely and be pure, to work in their homes, to do good, and to be submissive to their husbands. Then they will not bring shame on the word of God.
NLT.6: In the same way, encourage the young men to live wisely.
NLT.7: And you yourself must be an example to them by doing good works of every kind. Let everything you do reflect the integrity and seriousness of your teaching.
NLT.8: Teach the truth so that your teaching can’t be criticized. Then those who oppose us will be ashamed and have nothing bad to say about us.
NLT.9: Slaves must always obey their masters and do their best to please them. They must not talk back
NLT.10: or steal, but must show themselves to be entirely trustworthy and good. Then they will make the teaching about God our Savior attractive in every way.
NLT.11: For the grace of God has been revealed, bringing salvation to all people.
NLT.12: And we are instructed to turn from godless living and sinful pleasures. We should live in this evil world with wisdom, righteousness, and devotion to God,
NLT.13: while we look forward with hope to that wonderful day when the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ, will be revealed.
NLT.14: He gave his life to free us from every kind of sin, to cleanse us, and to make us his very own people, totally committed to doing good deeds.
NLT.15: You must teach these things and encourage the believers to do them. You have the authority to correct them when necessary, so don’t let anyone disregard what you say.

WEB.1: But say the things which fit sound doctrine,
WEB.2: that older men should be temperate, sensible, sober minded, sound in faith, in love, and in perseverance:
WEB.3: and that older women likewise be reverent in behavior, not slanderers nor enslaved to much wine, teachers of that which is good;
WEB.4: that they may train the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,
WEB.5: to be sober minded, chaste, workers at home, kind, being in subjection to their own husbands, that God’s word may not be blasphemed.
WEB.6: Likewise, exhort the younger men to be sober minded;
WEB.7: in all things showing yourself an example of good works; in your teaching showing integrity, seriousness, incorruptibility,
WEB.8: and soundness of speech that can’t be condemned; that he who opposes you may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say about us.
WEB.9: Exhort servants to be in subjection to their own masters, and to be well-pleasing in all things; not contradicting;
WEB.10: not stealing, but showing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God, our Savior, in all things.
WEB.11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,
WEB.12: instructing us to the intent that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we would live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present world;
WEB.13: looking for the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ;
WEB.14: who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify for himself a people for his own possession, zealous for good works.
WEB.15: Say these things and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no man despise you.

CSB17.1: But you are to proclaim things consistent with sound teaching. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.2: Older men <sup>ⓘ</sup> are to be self-controlled, worthy of respect, sensible, and sound in faith, love, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and endurance.
CSB17.3: In the same way, older women <sup>ⓚ</sup> are to be reverent in behavior, not slanderers, not slaves to excessive drinking. They are to teach what is good,
CSB17.4: so that they may encourage the young women to love their husbands and to love their children,
CSB17.5: to be self-controlled, pure, workers at home, kind, and in submission <sup>ⓛ</sup> to their husbands, so that God’s word <sup>ⓜ</sup> will not be slandered.
CSB17.6: In the same way, encourage the young men <sup>ⓝ</sup> to be self-controlled
CSB17.7: in everything. Make yourself an example of good works with integrity and dignity <sup>[1]</sup> in your teaching.
CSB17.8: Your message is to be sound beyond reproach, <sup>ⓐ</sup> so that any opponent will be ashamed, because he doesn’t have anything bad to say about us.
CSB17.9: Slaves are to submit to their masters <sup>ⓑ</sup> in everything, and to be well-pleasing, <sup>ⓒ</sup> not talking back
CSB17.10: or stealing, but demonstrating utter faithfulness, so that they may adorn the teaching of God our Savior <sup>ⓓ</sup> in everything.
CSB17.11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation <sup>[2]</sup> for all people, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.12: instructing us to deny godlessness <sup>ⓕ</sup> and worldly lusts <sup>ⓖ</sup> and to live in a sensible, righteous, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and godly <sup>ⓘ</sup> way in the present age,
CSB17.13: while we wait for the blessed hope, <sup>ⓙ</sup> the appearing of the glory <sup>ⓚ</sup> of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
CSB17.14: He gave himself for us <sup>ⓛ</sup> to redeem us from all lawlessness and to cleanse for himself a people for his own possession, <sup>ⓜ</sup> eager to do good works.
CSB17.15: Proclaim these things; encourage and rebuke with all authority. Let no one disregard <sup>[3]</sup> you.

AMP.1: But as for you, teach the things which are in agreement with sound doctrine [which produces men and women of good character whose lifestyle identifies them as true Christians].
AMP.2: Older men are to be temperate, dignified, sensible, sound in faith, in love, in steadfastness [Christlike in character].
AMP.3: Older women similarly are to be reverent in their behavior, not malicious gossips nor addicted to much wine, teaching what is right and good,
AMP.4: so that they may encourage the young women to tenderly love their husbands and their children,
AMP.5: to be sensible, pure, makers of a home [where God is honored], good-natured, being subject to their own husbands, so that the word of God will not be dishonored.
AMP.6: In a similar way urge the young men to be sensible and self-controlled and to behave wisely [taking life seriously].
AMP.7: And in all things show yourself to be an example of good works, with purity in doctrine [having the strictest regard for integrity and truth], dignified,
AMP.8: sound and beyond reproach in instruction, so that the opponent [of the faith] will be shamed, having nothing bad to say about us.
AMP.9: Urge bond-servants to be subject to their own masters in everything, to be pleasing and not talk back,
AMP.10: not stealing [things, regardless of value], but proving themselves trustworthy, so that in every respect they will adorn and do credit to the teaching of God our Savior.
AMP.11: For the [remarkable, undeserved] grace of God that brings salvation has appeared to all men.
AMP.12: It teaches us to reject ungodliness and worldly (immoral) desires, and to live sensible, upright, and godly lives [lives with a purpose that reflect spiritual maturity] in this present age,
AMP.13: awaiting and confidently expecting the [fulfillment of our] blessed hope and the glorious appearing of our great God and Savior, Christ Jesus,
AMP.14: who [willingly] gave Himself [to be crucified] on our behalf to redeem us and purchase our freedom from all wickedness, and to purify for Himself a chosen and very special people to be His own possession, who are enthusiastic for doing what is good.
AMP.15: Tell them these things. Encourage and rebuke with full authority. Let no one disregard or despise you [conduct yourself and your teaching so as to command respect].

MSG.1: Your job is to speak out on the things that make for solid doctrine.
MSG.2: Guide older men into lives of temperance, dignity, and wisdom, into healthy faith, love, and endurance.
MSG.3: Guide older women into lives of reverence so they end up as neither gossips nor drunks, but models of goodness.
MSG.4: By looking at them, the younger women will know how to love their husbands and children,
MSG.5: be virtuous and pure, keep a good house, be good wives. We don't want anyone looking down on God's Message because of their behavior.
MSG.6: Also, guide the young men to live disciplined lives.
MSG.7: But mostly, show them all this by doing it yourself, incorruptible in your teaching,
MSG.8: your words solid and sane. Then anyone who is dead set against us, when he finds nothing weird or misguided, might eventually come around.
MSG.9: Guide slaves into being loyal workers, a bonus to their masters—no back talk,
MSG.10: no petty thievery. Then their good character will shine through their actions, adding luster to the teaching of our Savior God.
MSG.11: God's readiness to give and forgive is now public. Salvation's available for everyone!
MSG.12: We're being shown how to turn our backs on a godless, indulgent life, and how to take on a God-filled, God-honoring life. This new life is starting right now,
MSG.13: and is whetting our appetites for the glorious day when our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ, appears.
MSG.14: He offered himself as a sacrifice to free us from a dark, rebellious life into this good, pure life, making us a people he can be proud of, energetic in goodness.
MSG.15: Tell them all this. Build up their courage, and discipline them if they get out of line. You're in charge. Don't let anyone put you down.

NIV.1:
What Must Be Taught to Various Groups
You must teach what is in accord with sound doctrine.
NIV.2: Teach the older men to be temperate, worthy of respect, self-controlled, and sound in faith, in love and in endurance.
NIV.3: Likewise, teach the older women to be reverent in the way they live, not to be slanderers or addicted to much wine, but to teach what is good.
NIV.4: Then they can train the younger women to love their husbands and children,
NIV.5: to be self-controlled and pure, to be busy at home, to be kind, and to be subject to their husbands, so that no one will malign the word of God.
NIV.6: Similarly, encourage the young men to be self-controlled.
NIV.7: In everything set them an example by doing what is good. In your teaching show integrity, seriousness
NIV.8: and soundness of speech that cannot be condemned, so that those who oppose you may be ashamed because they have nothing bad to say about us.
NIV.9: Teach slaves to be subject to their masters in everything, to try to please them, not to talk back to them,
NIV.10: and not to steal from them, but to show that they can be fully trusted, so that in every way they will make the teaching about God our Savior attractive.
NIV.11: For the grace of God that brings salvation has appeared to all men.
NIV.12: It teaches us to say “No” to ungodliness and worldly passions, and to live self-controlled, upright and godly lives in this present age,
NIV.13: while we wait for the blessed hope—the glorious appearing of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ,
NIV.14: who gave himself for us to redeem us from all wickedness and to purify for himself a people that are his very own, eager to do what is good.
NIV.15: These, then, are the things you should teach. Encourage and rebuke with all authority. Do not let anyone despise you.

NRSVCE.1: But as for you, teach what is consistent with sound doctrine.
NRSVCE.2: Tell the older men to be temperate, serious, prudent, and sound in faith, in love, and in endurance.
NRSVCE.3: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in behavior, not to be slanderers or slaves to drink; they are to teach what is good,
NRSVCE.4: so that they may encourage the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,
NRSVCE.5: to be self-controlled, chaste, good managers of the household, kind, being submissive to their husbands, so that the word of God may not be discredited.
NRSVCE.6: Likewise, urge the younger men to be self-controlled.
NRSVCE.7: Show yourself in all respects a model of good works, and in your teaching show integrity, gravity,
NRSVCE.8: and sound speech that cannot be censured; then any opponent will be put to shame, having nothing evil to say of us.
NRSVCE.9: Tell slaves to be submissive to their masters and to give satisfaction in every respect; they are not to talk back,
NRSVCE.10: not to pilfer, but to show complete and perfect fidelity, so that in everything they may be an ornament to the doctrine of God our Savior.
NRSVCE.11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all,
NRSVCE.12: training us to renounce impiety and worldly passions, and in the present age to live lives that are self-controlled, upright, and godly,
NRSVCE.13: while we wait for the blessed hope and the manifestation of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.14: He it is who gave himself for us that he might redeem us from all iniquity and purify for himself a people of his own who are zealous for good deeds.
NRSVCE.15: Declare these things; exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one look down on you.

NET.1: But as for you, communicate the behavior that goes with sound teaching.
NET.2: Older men are to be temperate, dignified, self-controlled, sound in faith, in love, and in endurance.
NET.3: Older women likewise are to exhibit behavior fitting for those who are holy, not slandering, not slaves to excessive drinking, but teaching what is good.
NET.4: In this way they will train the younger women to love their husbands, to love their children,
NET.5: to be self-controlled, pure, fulfilling their duties at home, kind, being subject to their own husbands, so that the message of God may not be discredited.
NET.6: Encourage younger men likewise to be self-controlled,
NET.7: showing yourself to be an example of good works in every way. In your teaching show integrity, dignity,
NET.8: and a sound message that cannot be criticized, so that any opponent will be at a loss, because he has nothing evil to say about us.
NET.9: Slaves are to be subject to their own masters in everything, to do what is wanted and not talk back,
NET.10: not pilfering, but showing all good faith, in order to bring credit to the teaching of God our Savior in everything.
NET.11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people.
NET.12: It trains us to reject godless ways and worldly desires and to live self-controlled, upright, and godly lives in the present age,
NET.13: as we wait for the happy fulfillment of our hope in the glorious appearing of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
NET.14: He gave himself for us to set us free from every kind of lawlessness and to purify for himself a people who are truly his, who are eager to do good.
NET.15: So communicate these things with the sort of exhortation or rebuke that carries full authority. Don’t let anyone look down on you.
